name: Persistent (immutable) balanced BST
task: Create /tmp/eval_pbst/pbst.py with three functions providing a persistent balanced BST (red-black or AVL): empty() returns an empty tree; insert(tree, key) returns a NEW tree containing key (the input tree must be unchanged — older versions remain queryable forever); contains(tree, key) returns True/False. The BST must remain balanced — operations must be O(log n). 10000 ascending inserts must complete in under 3 seconds. Crucially, insert must use path-copying (structural sharing): each new version copies only the O(log n) nodes on the root-to-leaf path and shares the rest with the previous version — so retaining ALL intermediate versions costs O(n log n) total nodes, NOT O(n^2). A full-tree copy on every insert is forbidden (it blows up memory). contains() on any historical version must work in O(log n). Treat keys as comparable integers.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, time, tracemalloc, random; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_pbst/pbst.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); t0 = mod.empty(); assert not mod.contains(t0, 0); t1 = mod.insert(t0, 5); assert mod.contains(t1, 5); assert not mod.contains(t0, 5), 'persistence violated: t0 changed'; t2 = mod.insert(t1, 3); t3 = mod.insert(t2, 7); assert mod.contains(t3, 3) and mod.contains(t3, 5) and mod.contains(t3, 7); assert mod.contains(t1, 5) and not mod.contains(t1, 3) and not mod.contains(t1, 7), 'old version contaminated'; t = mod.empty(); checkpoints = []; start = time.time()
for i in range(10000):
    t = mod.insert(t, i)
    if i in (0, 100, 1000, 5000, 9999): checkpoints.append((i, t))
elapsed = time.time() - start; assert elapsed < 3.0, f'10000 inserts took {elapsed:.2f}s — likely unbalanced'
for i in range(10000): assert mod.contains(t, i)
for cp_i, cp_t in checkpoints:
    for k in range(0, cp_i + 1): assert mod.contains(cp_t, k), f'v@{cp_i} missing {k}'
    if cp_i + 1 < 10000: assert not mod.contains(cp_t, cp_i + 1), f'v@{cp_i} has future key {cp_i+1}'
# MEMORY gate — the true discriminator. Retain EVERY version; path-copying is O(n log n) total nodes, full-tree copy is O(n^2) and blows the bound.
N = 8000; keys = list(range(N)); random.Random(1).shuffle(keys)
tracemalloc.start(); tracemalloc.clear_traces()
vt = mod.empty(); versions = []
for k in keys:
    vt = mod.insert(vt, k); versions.append(vt)
cur, peak = tracemalloc.get_traced_memory(); tracemalloc.stop()
peak_mb = peak / 1e6
assert peak_mb < 60.0, f'peak memory {peak_mb:.1f}MB retaining {N} versions — full-tree copy per insert is O(n^2); use path-copying (structural sharing)'
# spot-check a few retained versions still answer correctly (persistence + sharing intact)
for vi in (0, N//2, N-1):
    ver = versions[vi]; present = set(keys[:vi+1])
    for probe in (keys[0], keys[vi], keys[min(vi+1, N-1)]):
        assert mod.contains(ver, probe) == (probe in present), f'version {vi} wrong on {probe}'
print('OK')
